posts.text:
As sea levels rise along Indonesia’s coastline, areas in Java are sinking by 1.15 metres a year. The island’s subsidence is partly the legacy of centuries of exploitation.

Photos by Garry Lotulung detail how history and nature are colliding https://www.economist.com/interactive/1843/2026/06/12/the-amphibious-villagers-of-indonesia?taid=6a2bf2a0c0a91f00013414e4&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist
